title: The Café Welcome
characterDescription: A friendly young giant panda in a yellow t-shirt and blue jeans, with a warm round face, bright eyes, and a soft natural smile.
sceneDescription: Maple walks into a sunlit café and greets three animal friends, locking eyes with each one in turn for two full seconds before sitting down.
annotation: Notice how Maple holds eye contact for two seconds with each friend before moving to the next. This sweep & hold pattern signals genuine recognition vs perfunctory greeting — every friend feels individually seen, not collectively acknowledged.

mainVideo.prompt: Pixar-style 3D animation in the visual tone of Kung Fu Panda. Maple, a friendly young giant panda in a yellow t-shirt and blue jeans, walks into a sunlit cozy café with warm color palette and soft golden hour lighting. He sees three animal friends — a rabbit, a fox, and a meerkat — seated at a wooden round table. Beat 1 (0-5s): Maple approaches the table and locks eyes first with the rabbit on the left, holding the gaze with a warm smile. Beat 2 (5-10s): Maple's gaze sweeps right to the fox in the middle, holds for two seconds with a widening smile. Beat 3 (10-15s): Maple completes the sweep to the meerkat on the right, smiles, then waves with both hands and says 'Hey friends!' in a warm English voice. Acoustic café background music with ambient cup-clinking. Medium-wide shot with a slight handheld feel.

observationGuide.headline: How Maple earns instant trust with the sweep & hold technique

moments.what: Maple locks eyes with the first friend before reaching the table.
moments.why: Establishes connection before proximity, signaling intentionality.

moments.what: His gaze deliberately moves left to right without skipping anyone.
moments.why: Each friend feels individually seen, not collectively greeted.

moments.what: Each gaze lock lasts a full two seconds before moving on.
moments.why: Two seconds is the minimum for the brain to register real attention.

moments.what: Smile widens at the moment of eye contact, then voice and hands join in.
moments.why: Layering eyes + smile + voice + hands creates a full-spectrum welcome.

practiceSteps.instruction: Before you reach the room, pick the first person you'll greet. Lock eyes with them as you walk toward them, holding the gaze for at least two seconds.
practiceSteps.tip: Don't break gaze until they acknowledge you back.
practiceSteps.videoPrompt: Pixar 3D close-up: Maple the panda walking toward camera in a sunlit café, eyes locked forward with a warm focused gaze. Five seconds of confident approach, no speech, subtle ambient café sounds.
practiceSteps.imagePrompt: Minimalist sketch of a friendly panda character mid-stride, eyes locked forward in a warm gaze, gentle smile, confident posture. White background, no text.

practiceSteps.instruction: Move your eyes deliberately from one person to the next. Don't skip anyone. The motion should feel intentional, not scanning.
practiceSteps.tip: Imagine you're painting each face with your gaze before moving on.
practiceSteps.videoPrompt: Pixar 3D medium shot of Maple's face from chest up. His eyes deliberately move left, hold, then right, hold, then center, hold. Each hold lasts about a second. Soft background blur, no speech.
practiceSteps.imagePrompt: Minimalist sketch of a panda character's face in medium shot, eyes mid-transition between left and right, eyebrows relaxed, soft smile. White background, no text.

practiceSteps.instruction: When your eyes lock with someone, count one Mississippi, two Mississippi before moving on. Two full seconds is the minimum.
practiceSteps.tip: If two seconds feels too long, focus on noticing one detail about their face.
practiceSteps.videoPrompt: Pixar 3D tight close-up of Maple the panda's face, eyes locked directly into camera. He holds the gaze still for five full seconds, blinking softly, with a calm warm expression. No speech.
practiceSteps.imagePrompt: Minimalist sketch of a panda's face in tight close-up, eyes locked directly at viewer with a sustained warm gaze. White background, no text.

practiceSteps.instruction: At the moment your eyes lock, let your smile widen by a fraction. Don't smile before — let the eye contact trigger the smile.
practiceSteps.tip: The smile should arrive a fraction of a second after the eye lock, never before.
practiceSteps.videoPrompt: Pixar 3D close-up of Maple's face. His eyes lock onto the viewer, hold for one beat, then his smile slowly widens into a Duchenne smile that crinkles the eyes. No speech, soft chime sound.
practiceSteps.imagePrompt: Minimalist sketch of a panda character's face mid-smile, eyes already locked with viewer, smile blossoming with crinkled eyes. White background, no text.
